items.title: Huge loan needed to finance fuel subsidies
items.desc: Thailand's Oil Fuel Fund is seeking a loan of up to 150 billion baht to stabilise its finances and continue subsidising domestic fuel prices as global diesel costs soar, according to Prasert Sinsukprasert, energy permanent secretary.

items.title: CP unveils university partnership to transform education
items.desc: Charoen Pokphand (CP) Group has partnered with Xi'an Jiaotong-Liverpool University (XJTLU) to launch a "syntegrative" education centre that can disrupt the industry in Thailand, positioning the nation as a global learning hub.

items.title: Lawmaker pensions stir big debate
items.desc: Lawmakers have expressed mixed views over scrapping a pension fund for former MPs and senators, amid concerns over fairness and long-term sustainability.

items.title: Ex-MP wants concession  deal probe
items.desc: Former Democrat MP Chanchai Issarasenarak has petitioned the National Anti‑Corruption Commission (NACC) to investigate Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul, the cabinet and the board of Airports of Thailand (AoT) over amendments to duty‑free concession contracts that he claims caused up to 180 billion baht in losses to the state.

items.title: Energy Ministry battens down amid 'worst crisis'
items.desc: The Energy Ministry's permanent secretary, Prasert Sinsukprasert, has described the current energy situation as one of the most severe crises in Thailand's history.

items.title: PTG denies fuel profiteering allegations
items.desc: PTG Energy, a SET-listed oil trader under the PT brand, has dismissed speculation that it was hoarding fuel for profit, adding it has no plans to pursue legal action against those spreading the accusations.

items.title: Liquidity pressures rise in Thai bond market
items.desc: The corporate bond market faces a critical test this year, as heightened refinancing risks amid a slowing economy and escalating geopolitical tensions threaten roughly 687 billion baht in bonds set to mature over the remaining three quarters, according to the Thai Bond Market Association (ThaiBMA).

items.title: Oil refineries in the spotlight
items.desc: A proposal for the government to intervene in oil refinery costs and profits was not included as one of the seven measures recently unveiled to ease the consumer impact of surging global crude oil prices.

items.title: First US fighter jet shot down over Iran
items.desc: A ​US fighter jet has been shot ​down over ‌Iran and a search and rescue operation was under way for any survivors, a US official said on Friday, in the first such known incident since the the war began on Feb 28.

items.title: Possible security services link to MP’s shooting probed
items.desc: Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul has ordered a swift investigation into allegations that a vehicle used in an attack on a southern MP was linked to the Internal Security Operations Command (Isoc), as authorities move to clarify the circumstances surrounding the incident.

items.title: Korean loan shark gang leader arrested in Pathum Thani
items.desc: PATHUM THANI - Police have arrested the alleged leader of a South Korean illegal lending network accused of defrauding more than 9,000 victims out of over 355 million baht, after he fled to Thailand to evade prosecution.

items.title: Kalasin beauty queen loses 2.6m baht to ‘occult’ scam
items.desc: KHON KAEN: A provincial beauty queen is pursuing legal action after allegedly being duped into spending more than 2.6 million baht on amulets and rituals promised to restore her love life.

items.title: Cambodia passes landmark cybercrime law
items.desc: PHNOM PENH - Cambodia’s ⁠parliament on Friday passed the ​first law dedicated to targeting scam centres accused of bilking international victims out of billions ​of dollars, as pressure mounts on governments ‌to tackle the illicit businesses.

items.title: 57 million litres of fuel ‘missing’ from Surat Thani depots
items.desc: Irregularities have been found in the disappearance of 57 million litres of fuel during transport to six oil depots in the southern province of Surat Thani, according to Justice Minister Rutthaphon Naowarat.

items.title: Post Malone returns to Bangkok with 'Big Ass World Tour'
items.desc: The 9x diamond-certified global superstar (and one of the most-streamed artists globally to boot) Post Malone will return to Asia in September and October with his "Big Ass World Tour", with special guest Don Toliver.

items.title: Cement truck falls from bridge onto Bangna-Trat highway
items.desc: SAMUT PRAKAN – A cement mixer truck crashed through the barrier of a U-turn bridge and plunged onto the Bangna-Trat Highway (Theparat Road) on Friday afternoon, blocking two lanes of traffic and leaving its driver critically injured.

items.title: Guru's Weekly Buzz: April 3-9
items.desc: Guru By Bangkok Post's weekly pick of the most exciting products, activities, food and travel to indulge in.

items.title: PM orders DSI to probe suspected oil hoarding
items.desc: Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul on Friday revealed evidence of widespread oil hoarding and market manipulation, pledging swift legal action and ordering a special investigation to expand the probe to those involved.

items.title: Your horoscope for April 3-9
items.desc: Your spot-on horoscope for work, money and relationship from Guru by the Bangkok Post's famously accurate fortune teller. Let's see how you will fare this week and beyond.

items.title: MPC member vacates seat amid reports to join finance minister
items.desc: A member of the Bank of Thailand’s Monetary Policy Committee (MPC), Santitarn Sathirathai, has left the seven-member panel amid reports he is set to join a team led by Finance Minister Ekniti Nitithanpraphas.

items.title: Myanmar charts the path after 2025 election amid lingering crisis
items.desc: Political Developments in Myanmar

items.title: Human remains found on Thai cargo ship attacked in Strait of Hormuz
items.desc: Human remains were found within the affected area of the ​vessel ⁠MV Mayuree Naree in the Strait of ‌Hormuz, which was attacked last month during ⁠the Middle East conflict, ⁠Thailand's Precious Shipping ​reported on ​Friday.

items.title: Thai regulator tightens market oversight to curb 'grey capital'
items.desc: Thailand’s ⁠Securities and Exchange ​Commission (SEC) is stepping up measures ​to curb ‌grey capital, mule accounts and investment scams, calling it a national priority.

items.title: 谁动了泰国5700万升燃油？政府下令彻查
items.desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 4月3日，泰国总理兼内政部长阿努廷召集司法、国防、能源、海事、海关、税务、警方及特别案件调查厅（DSI）等多个部门召开联合新闻发布会，通报泰国国内能源安全形势，揭露当前市场存在的燃油囤积、投放拖延、非法转运及大量燃油“失踪”等严重问题，并宣布启动全面彻查行动，坚决维护国家能源安全与民众切身利益。 发布会上，阿努廷明确表态，针对各类扰乱燃油市场秩序、危害国家能源安全的违法行为，泰国政府将严查到底、绝不姑息。他已正式指示特别案件调查厅，将相关燃油违法案件列为特别案件，开展全方位、深层次调查。阿努廷强调，保障国内燃油供应稳定、防止燃油短缺，是当前政府核心工作之一，对于囤积燃油、拖延供油、非法转运等违规违法行为，必将予以严厉打击。 为全面管控燃油市场、追查违法线索，泰国政府已专门成立燃油供应监测中心，并依据《1973年燃油短缺防治紧急法令》，授予相关执法部门全面执法权限，对所有影响国家能源安全、损害民众利益的行为展开逐一追查。阿努廷着重表示，此次调查将坚守“不看身份，只看行为”的原则，无论涉案人员具备何种身份背景、拥有多大势力，只要存在借机牟利、侵害民生、破坏国家能源稳定的行为，都将依法受到严惩，绝不法外开恩。 据阿努廷介绍，近期，泰国司法部、特别案件调查厅、国家警察总署、能源商业厅、国家海洋利益维护中心、海事厅、消费税厅、内贸厅、海关厅及地方各级行政部门，已对国内大中型

items.title: 泰总理：别抢油！宋干节不会缺油
items.desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 4月3日，泰国总理阿努廷就当前国内燃油供应情况作出说明。他表示，政府已提前做好应对准备，确保宋干节假期期间全国燃油供应正常，呼吁民众不必恐慌，也无需额外囤积燃油。 阿努廷指出，随着宋干节临近，返乡和出行需求将大幅上升，政府已提前统筹相关资源，并持续与能源部门及企业协调，保障节日期间民众能正常加油、顺利出行。 他坦言，政府最担心的并非燃油储备不足，而是部分民众因担忧而提前抢购、囤积燃油。如果将油加进汽车油箱尚可理解，但若装入油桶或油罐中储存，反而可能造成局部加油站出现临时性供应紧张。 为此，阿努廷呼吁民众保持理性，按平常习惯正常加油即可，无需过度储备。他强调，只要全社会维持正常的消费节奏，宋干节期间就不会出现所谓的“油荒”。 据悉，政府已依据相关能源法律，提前协调经营单位在节日期间安排油罐运输车待命，并适度放宽部分运输管理规定。一旦有加油站出现短时缺油，可迅速补充供应，避免影响民众返乡和旅游出行。 此外，阿努廷表示，政府将持续严查借机哄抬价格、违法牟利或损害消费者权益的行为，维护市场秩序，保障民众合法权益。 在整体能源安全方面，阿努廷透露，尽管泰国依赖进口原油，但国家层面已做好供应规划，相关燃油采购订单已安排至今年6月初，整体供应处于可控状态。他进一步指出，泰国目前每日炼油能力维持在较高水平，足以支撑国内日常及节日期间的燃油需求，民众无需对节日期间的油料供应产生过度

items.title: 宋干节出行注意！廊曼机场提醒：至少提前3小时到机场
items.desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 随着泰国宋干节假期临近，曼谷廊曼国际机场于4月3日发布出行提示，建议计划在节日期间乘机出行的旅客，至少提前2至3小时抵达机场办理手续，以免因客流高峰耽误行程。 据机场方面预测，4月10日至19日出行高峰期间，廊曼机场预计将迎送旅客约106万人次，日均约10万人次；同期计划起降航班6551架次，整体客流量较平日显著上升。 机场管理方表示，4月11日预计将成为节日期间最为繁忙的一天。届时，无论是国内航班还是国际航班，部分时段均可能出现旅客集中排队现象。根据预测，机场主要高峰时段为： •  上午5:00至7:00   •  上午9:00至11:00   •  下午3:00至6:00    为减少排队等候时间，机场方面建议旅客尽量提前完成线上值机，并在出发前再次确认航班状态与具体时间，以便合理规划行程。 作为泰国年度最重要的出行高峰之一，宋干节期间航空、铁路及公路运输系统均将面临较大压力。廊曼机场呼吁旅客尽早出门，为安检、托运及登机等环节预留充足时间，确保顺利出行。 （编译：WAN；来源：曼谷邮报） The post 宋干节出行注意！廊曼机场提醒：至少提前3小时到机场 appeared first on 泰国头条新闻 .

items.title: Phuket tuk tuk driver accuses Russian man of refusing to pay for service
items.desc: Phuket tuk tuk driver accuses Russian man of refusing to pay for service Legacy A Phuket tuk tuk driver accused a foreign passenger of refusing to pay and then claiming the driver overcharged him at 3,000 baht. The driver, who operates in Patong in Kathu district, shared a video on social media showing him confronting the passenger in a hotel lobby. He said he may start collecting fares in advance to prevent passengers from leaving without paying. In the video, the driver heard saying… “He (the foreign passenger) said okay before the service but refused to pay me upon arrival.&#822

items.title: Thai central bank chief says no drastic policy adjustment needed at the moment
items.desc: Thailand's central bank chief ​said there was no need for a drastic ​monetary policy adjustment at the moment, ‌despite expectations of a rise in inflation driven by higher oil prices amid the war in the Middle East.

items.title: Thailand’s April heat can kill, but it can also bankrupt you
items.desc: Thailand’s April heat can kill, but it can also bankrupt you Legacy The heat in Thailand in April is not just uncomfortable. It is, by measurable standards, medically dangerous. Air temperatures regularly hit 40°C and above, but the number that actually matters is the heat index , which is how hot it feels when humidity is factored in. In Bangkok, that figure climbs to around 53°C on an average April afternoon. The reason April hits harder than any other month is timing: Thailand’s northeast monsoon ends around mid-February, but the cooling rains of the southwest monsoon don’

items.title: Thai Airways allows two power banks, bans onboard use
items.desc: Thai Airways allows two power banks, bans onboard use Legacy Thai Airways International issued a security notice today, April 3, outlining new power bank rules that allow each passenger to carry up to two devices on board, but prohibit their use and charging during flights. The airline said in a social media post that the restriction has been in effect since March 27, in line with safety regulations announced by the International Civil Aviation Organisation (ICAO). The rule applies to all flights and limits each passenger to a maximum of two portable power banks. Thai Airways added that using

items.title: 泰航发布充电宝携带新规：每人限带2个
items.desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 4月3日，泰国国际航空公司（Thai Airways，简称“泰航”） 通过官方Facebook账号 “Thai Airways” 发布最新安全公告，进一步明确旅客携带充电宝（Power Bank）登机的相关规定。 公告指出，上述措施依据国际民航组织（ICAO）最新航空安全要求制定，旨在降低锂电池类产品在航空运输过程中可能带来的安全风险。相关规定已于2026年3月27日正式生效，具体如下： 1.每位乘客最多可携带2个充电宝登机；   2.飞行全程严禁使用充电宝，亦不得在机上为充电宝充电。    泰航提醒广大旅客，为确保航班运行安全及飞行秩序，请在出行前提前检查随身携带物品，并严格遵守航空安全规定，以免影响登机安排。 值得关注的是，类似规定近期也已在部分地区同步收紧。此前，中国香港国际机场在新规实施首日，就有不少旅客表示对“每人限带2个充电宝”的要求并不知情。 据相关报道，不少从香港出发的旅客反映，他们事前并未获悉有关新规已正式实施，而在规定生效初期，机场现场与值机区域内的相关提醒标识也相对有限，导致部分旅客在出行时对新要求准备不足。 在当前全球航空业持续强化锂电池类物品运输管理的背景下，旅客在搭乘国际或地区航班前，建议提前查阅航空公司及机场方面发布的最新行李与安全规定，以避免因携带限制变化而影响行程。 （编辑：WAN；来源：Thai A

items.title: Buriram man accuses high school teacher of sexually assaulting and manipulating him
items.desc: Buriram man accuses high school teacher of sexually assaulting and manipulating him Legacy A male teacher in Buriram province was accused of physically abusing and sexually grooming a schoolboy for more than four years. The victim’s sister, 35 year old Pitchaman, accompanied her brother, identified only as M, to Buriram Provincial Police Office to follow up on the complaint filed with Mueang Buriram Police Station six months ago. The siblings later shared details of the case with Channel 8 in the hope of prompting progress in the investigation. M said he enrolled in an extra English clas

items.title: 在中国广西崖壁上的书店你见过吗？
items.desc: 傍晚时分，太阳落下山头。位于中国广西河池市罗城县的棉花天坑崖壁上，一盏盏暖黄色的灯光渐次亮起，将悬崖书店瞬间照亮，游客惊叹声、拍照快门声此起彼伏。“武侠小说里的藏经阁具象化了！”有网友这样评价。 棉花天坑深达326米，峭壁陡立。在这地质奇观中，悬于半空的悬崖书店，通过木制栈道与观景台相连，成为景区上千米栈道途中独特的栖息地。白天，游客在此歇脚、阅读；夜晚，灯光璀璨，铺展浪漫与想象。（来源：广西日报） 棉花天坑景区内的悬崖书店，以其独特的建筑与阅读体验，吸引众多游人读者前来躲避酷暑、汲取知识、拍照打卡 龙涛 摄 夜幕下的悬崖书店，灯光璀璨，铺展浪漫与想象 王希 摄 The post 在中国广西崖壁上的书店你见过吗？ appeared first on 泰国头条新闻 .

items.title: MP pushes back against Clean Air Bill as Thais are ‘breathing dust to death’
items.desc: MP pushes back against Clean Air Bill as Thais are ‘breathing dust to death’ Legacy Thai netizens, today, April 3, slammed objections to the Clean Air Bill , which were raised in Parliament, as people in North Thailand continued to face severe PM2.5 pollution . The issue stemmed from remarks made by Bhumjaithai Party list MP Supachai Jaisamut during a House of Representatives meeting on April 1, when he raised concerns about the draft Clean Air Bill, which is awaiting further action from the new Cabinet. Supachai said the draft bill should not be rushed, arguing that Thailand’s air

items.title: Human remains found on Thai cargo ship attacked in Hormuz
items.desc: Human remains found on Thai cargo ship attacked in Hormuz Legacy Human remains were found aboard the Mayuree Naree cargo ship during a second search for three missing Thai crew members following the Strait of Hormuz attack on March 11. The Iranian military attacked the Thai cargo ship after it left the United Arab Emirates for India on March 11, stating that the vessel had ignored a ban on travel through the strait during escalating conflict involving Israel and US coalition forces. Precious Shipping maintained that it was in contact with relevant departments before the vessel travelled throug

items.title: 泰国公主诗琳通将访华
items.desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 4月3日，外交部发言人宣布：应中国政府邀请，泰王国玛哈扎克里·诗琳通公主殿下将于4月4日至11日访问中国。 （来源：中国外交部） The post 泰国公主诗琳通将访华 appeared first on 泰国头条新闻 .

items.title: 3 foreigners caught using toy banknotes at Phuket entertainment venue
items.desc: 3 foreigners caught using toy banknotes at Phuket entertainment venue Legacy Police arrested three foreigners on April 1 after they attempted to settle a bill at an entertainment venue on Bangla Road in Phuket with toy banknotes . A staff member at the venue contacted police after detecting suspicious banknotes used by the group of foreign men, whose nationalities were not disclosed. The suspects reportedly handed over three US$100 notes to pay for food and drinks. The worker prevented the group from leaving and checked the banknotes more closely, later confirming they were fake. According to

items.title: Man caught cooking dog meat at Udon Thani home
items.desc: Man caught cooking dog meat at Udon Thani home Legacy Police in Udon Thani arrested a man in Chiang Yuen subdistrict yesterday, April 2, after a Facebook post led police to his home , where officers found him cooking dog meat . Officers from Huai Luang Subdistrict Police Station went to investigate the property at 11am. Upon arrival, they found the suspect, identified as 49 year old Gok, cooking meat in a pot for a local dish called hyoi. When questioned, the suspect reportedly admitted the meat came from a dog. According to officers, Gok said the animal had died after being hit by a vehicle.

items.title: cTrader Store Affiliate Programme: Two revenue streams within one extended IB model
items.desc: cTrader Store Affiliate Programme: Two revenue streams within one extended IB model Legacy With the cTrader Store Affiliate Programme , Spotware introduces a new approach to affiliate marketing. Partners can now benefit from two revenue streams instead of one and expand conversion opportunities beyond broker signups, while keeping traffic aligned with their existing setup. In this model, the cTrader Store complements the IB structure – it doesn’t replace it. Two revenue streams instead of one Nothing in the current IB structure changes, the programme just builds on it. Partners earn through tw

items.title: 泰国女演员遭性侵案嫌犯落网，警方已申请羁押
items.desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 4月3日上午，曼谷Khlong Tan警局警员将涉嫌性侵女演员的34岁男子尼功押送至曼谷南部刑事法院申请首次羁押。该男子涉嫌猥亵及性侵等相关罪名，目前警方已完成初步审讯程序。 据悉，该案件发生于3月31日凌晨，泰德混血模特兼演员克莉斯汀因服用药物产生不适反应，无法自主救援。随后，该男子以救援人员身份来到公寓，在克莉丝汀无法反抗的情况下对其实施了侵犯，并使用手机进行拍摄。4月1日，克莉丝汀第一时间前往Khlong Tan警局报案，并提交了事发时所穿的睡衣作为关键证据。 据警方通报，该男子涉嫌在受害人无法反抗的情况下实施猥亵与性侵行为，涉及威胁与暴力情节，曼谷南部刑事法院于4月1日签发逮捕令，目前案件仍在深入调查中。 在押送过程中，该男子全程未回应媒体提问，神情紧张。鉴于案件性质严重且舆论关注度高，警方已向法院明确提出反对保释，认为一旦释放，可能存在干扰证据及影响案件审理的风险。 （编译：jinda；审校：wan） The post 泰国女演员遭性侵案嫌犯落网，警方已申请羁押 appeared first on 泰国头条新闻 .

items.title: 泰国女Rapper MILLI惊艳韩国！斩获知名说唱比赛第四创历史
items.desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 近日，韩国人气说唱竞演节目Show Me The Money Season 12圆满落幕。泰国女Rapper MILLI成功闯入总决赛，成为节目史上首位晋级决赛的外国选手，并最终获得第四名，创下历史纪录。 在4月2日的决赛舞台上，MILLI先是在全球投票中斩获第一，随后带来极具冲击力的表演。她以“MSG”（Money Stage Glory / Master Still Growing）为主题，将泰式恰恰恰节奏融合进嘻哈音乐，呈现出独特且富有感染力的舞台效果。 演出中，韩国Rapper Omega Sapien惊喜助阵，“厨师MILLI”的创意造型与高能舞台设计相结合，进一步点燃现场氛围，展现出强烈的个人风格与国际化表达。 最终，在综合各渠道投票后，MILLI以第四名收官。本次成绩不仅实现个人突破，也标志着泰国音乐人在韩国主流说唱舞台上的重要进展。 （编译：jinda；审校：wan；来源：pptvhd36） The post 泰国女Rapper MILLI惊艳韩国！斩获知名说唱比赛第四创历史 appeared first on 泰国头条新闻 .

items.title: Thai woman hires friend to kill her ex-boyfriend before taking her life
items.desc: Thai woman hires friend to kill her ex-boyfriend before taking her life Legacy A Thai woman and her friend, who was in unrequited love with her, allegedly arranged the murder of her ex-boyfriend on the M6 motorway in Nakhon Ratchasima on March 28. The woman took her own life while escaping from police. The victim, 30 year old Suppakorn “Golf” Traiphopklang, was found dead on the motorway with multiple gunshot wounds to his body. Police reviewed CCTV footage and found that two suspects fled the scene in a white Mitsubishi Pajero SUV. Investigators later identified the suspects as 28 year old Ku

items.title: 泰国商业部多项目助力“本地好物走向全球”
items.desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 4月2日，泰国商业部国际贸易促进厅（DITP）表示，作为负责连接泰国业者与全球市场的部门，DITP计划以“THINK THAILAND NEXT LEVEL”为概念，通过各种项目和平台，持续推动泰国商品和服务升级。现如今，将新产品打造成世界级品牌不能只追求质量，还应该关注思维方式、产品开发，以及进入合适市场的时机。 为此，DITP推出“泰国好物走向全球”项目，帮助泰国潜力商品站上世界舞台，包括： 1. 组织“SMEs Pro-active”和“U-NEXT Exporter”等活动，让业者有机会交流开拓海外市场的经验，并支持他们参加国际贸易活动，增加建立网络和拓展全球业务的机会。 2. 通过“DEmark”和“Talent Thai & Designers’ Room”平台展示产品故事，帮助传统手工艺或地方产品经良好设计和包装，延伸成符合全球市场需求的Lifestyle商品。 3. 带领泰国商品参加THAIFEX – Anuga Asia、THAIFEX – HOREX Asia或Bangkok Gems & Jewelry Fair等展会，让泰国品牌有机会测试市场、接触真实买家和扩大业务。另外，通过Thaitrade.com平台提供持续接触外国客户的另一条渠道。 4. DITP还将举办工作坊和业者能力提升培训，例如： &#

items.title: 攀牙荣登全球“旅行者点评奖”第一名！
items.desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 4月2日，泰国安达曼海岸边的美丽天堂“攀牙”于近日力压全球众多知名城市，拿下了Booking.com“旅行者点评奖”（Traveller Review Awards 2026）第一名。这个成绩来自全球3.7亿条真实评论，充分证明泰国旅游业已经彻底摆脱“去了没感觉”的问题。“攀牙”把泰国人热情好客的DNA，与文化和美食等软实力完美结合，让游客感到安全、安心，仿佛回到了第二个家。豪华酒店到处都能盖，但这种真诚的感受和深入当地生活的体验，在别的地方找不到。 这项世界级殊荣并非偶然，背后是泰国旅游业一次重要的自我升级，通过“泰国魅力”与“未来科技”的巧妙融合，达到了非常自然的效果。Booking.com的主管指出，泰国业者很擅长从游客评论中听取意见，不断改进服务，总是能走在趋势前面。对于正渴望一场疗愈之旅、想要有意义且难忘的旅行体验，不妨收拾行李直接到攀牙去感受世界级的热情。 “旅行者点评奖”已经连续举办第14年，Booking.com汇集全球游客的真实评论，评选出带来难忘体验的旅游目的地，同时表扬令人印象深刻的服务提供商。2026年，泰国共有16692家旅游相关业者获奖，平均得分高达8.8分。 2026年“旅行者点评奖”前10名如下： 1. 泰国攀牙 2. 墨西哥伊达尔戈 3. 加拿大纽芬兰与拉布拉多 4. 西班牙纳瓦拉 5. 美国爱达荷 6. 印度喜马偕尔邦 7. 德
